Steps to reproduce: 1. Drag and drop valid TSV input 2. View file statistics 3. Click 'Start Harvest'
Expected result: File validates instantly, displays exact unique/invalid/duplicate count, and harvest begins without crashing
Actual result: File parses correctly using unified helper, stats match perfectly, pipeline engages seamlessly

Steps to reproduce: 1. Click Start Harvest 2. Click Pause 3. Click Resume 4. Click Cancel
Expected result: Harvest toggles states dynamically and UI updates corresponding button usability (e.g. Pause turns to Resume)
Actual result: Worker thread correctly pauses yield loop and gracefully interrupts upon Cancel.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Upload test validation TSV containing spaces, missing digits, and multiple identical lines
Expected result: Pre-harvest validates ISBNs exactly correctly, UI splits 'Loaded ISBNs' from total file rows, and live stats reflect exact processing count
Actual result: Accurately detected 3 unique valid rows minus 3 duplicates and 2 invalids.
Notes: Math formulas for counts were previously mismatched. Rewrote backend to use the exact same parser for both GUI and Database.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Load App (without file) 2. Upload TSV 3. Click 'Clear'
Expected result: Button is disabled gray initially, turns red 'DangerButton' instantly upon upload, completely clears UI and resets when clicked
Actual result: Button toggles states perfectly. Emoji/text scaling issue bypassed.
Notes: Had to fix OS-level text clipping by relying on generic DangerButton CSS instead of fixed 30x30 bounding box hacks.

Expected result: Pings LOC, Harvard, OpenLibrary domains simultaneously for latency tests

Expected result: Flexible layout components flex appropriately horizontally, errors are printed inline instead of spawning infinite popups
Actual result: Scroll area anchors and expands. Inline logging successfully avoids OS interrupt popups.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Launch GUI. 2. Toggle Theme Settings between Light and Dark. 3. Observe background and text elements.
Expected result: The application correctly toggles to High Contrast light backgrounds (#f3f4f6). All previously hardcoded inline styles calculate to #0f172a via ThemeManager.
Actual result: Styles dynamically update. Hardcoded .setStyleSheet overrides in Config, Targets, and Dashboard were successfully routed to standard CSS mappings.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Navigate to Config Tab. 2. Observe the Profile and LCCN Format Dropdown boxes. 3. Interact with the Target Retry Spinbox.
Expected result: Components render with perfectly sharp borders and transparent backgrounds overriding OS-level inner-shadow rendering artifacts.
Actual result: Reconstructed pseudo-classes (::drop-down, ::up-button) fixed the gray inner overlays; buttons now display exact palette tokens securely.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Start App. 2. Locate the ● IDLE badges in Dashboard and Harvest tabs. 3. Resize window boundaries horizontally.
Expected result: The pills maintain exactly 12px radius, remaining perfectly elliptical instead of warping into tall squares when Qts layout attempts to stretch them.
Actual result: Injected strict min-height and max-height constraints to StatusPills, locking their aspect ratios precisely.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Go to Dashboard Tab. 2. Observe Profile section gear icon.
Expected result: The icon floats securely with a true transparent background and zero CSS border constraints, merging seamlessly with the surface panel.
Actual result: Ripped out the green bounding box and transparency hex masks. Icon now accurately respects the active theme backdrop.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Feed 1 Valid ISBN into Harvest Tab. 2. Click Start. 3. Wait for run termination.
Expected result: The Action Banner explicitly triggers a brilliant full green background overlay with starkly contrasted font coloring based on the Theme.
Actual result: Added QFrame#HarvestBanner binding class; the background floods green, and explicitly casts child QLabel text to use the inverted background token for perfect legibility.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Start Harvest targeting 5 APIs. 2. Observe live progress bar label and text output.
Expected result: Intermediate checking [API] for [ISBN] emissions are suppressed from the main UI label to eliminate rapid flickering.
Actual result: Removed the target_start emit branch inside harvest_tab_v2.py. Live progress updates cleanly step between Found/Cache/Not Found milestones only.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Open Configuration tab 2. Examine 'Profile' and 'LCCN Format' dropdowns 3. Compare with 'Retry days' spinbox.
Expected result: Dropdowns have customized chevrons, expanded widths, helper text beneath them, and no native OS inner shadows or dark patches.
Actual result: Custom QComboBox css overrides default styling. Chevrons display correctly. Input controls use transparent backgrounds without dark artifacts.
Notes: Fixed hardcoded box-shadows and QSpinBox pseudo-elements (::up-button, ::down-button) to seamlessly blend with the active application surface.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Launch Application 2. Open Settings and toggle 'Light Mode' 3. Navigate through Dashboard, Targets, and Input tabs.
Expected result: All helper text, card titles, and progress labels switch to high-contrast dark colors. No invisible white text on white backgrounds.
Actual result: Labels natively inherit {text} properties from the global semantic dictionaries.
Notes: Stripped dozens of inline .setStyleSheet() overrides that were forcing purely Dark Mode string primitives onto Light Mode surfaces.

Steps to reproduce: 1. Initiate a small harvest. 2. Observe the 'IDLE/RUNNING' pills and the top banner 3. Wait for the run to conclude.
Expected result: Live logs exclude verbose target checking. Status pills retain a strict pill shape (12px height/radius). Banner transitions to a bold green background on Success.
Actual result: Signal emitters stripped the checking payloads. Status pills locked CSS heights against Qt's stretch. Banner explicitly targets QFrame#HarvestBanner to trigger {success} mode.
Notes: Wrapped completion metrics in bold tags for maximum contrast against the green background.
